source_pr.title: tikv: make region_request_test stable
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? old TestOnSendFailedWithCloseKnownStoreThenUseNewOne test is occasionally failed ``` [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] FAIL: region_request_test.go:92: testRegionRequestSuite.TestOnSendFailedWithCloseKnownStoreThenUseNewOne [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] region_request_test.go:127: [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] c.Assert(err, IsNil) [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] ... value *errors.withStack = [tikv:9002]TiKV server timeout ("[tikv:9002]TiKV server timeout") [2019-07-30T11:52:47.513Z] ``` the old test has a problem new store never be a leader and accept the request. and old assert will be failed to because of async scheduleReload + race/leak slow running. ### What is changed and how it works? elect a new leader to another store when one store fails ### Check List Tests - Unit test - Integration test Code changes - N/A Side effects - N/A Related changes - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/11541)

symptoms: and old assert will be failed to because of async scheduleReload + race/leak slow running.
failure_signature: and old assert will be failed to because of async scheduleReload + race/leak slow running.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to environment/external dependencies (resource contention, ports, or real cluster timing). This PR stabilizes it by: tikv: make region_request_test stable.

evidence: evidence: and old assert will be failed to because of async scheduleReload + race/leak slow running.
